title: Base64 Encode a String
description: Encodes a plain text string into Base64 format. The '-n' flag is crucial as it prevents 'echo' from appending a hidden newline character, which would drastically alter the resulting Base64 string.
when_to_use: Use this to encode payloads or credentials before inserting them into HTTP headers (like Basic Auth) or when passing complex bash payloads through restricted environments.
common_pitfalls: Forgetting the '-n' flag is the most common reason encoded payloads fail to execute on the target.

title: Base64 Decode a String
description: Reverses a Base64 encoded string back into plaintext.
when_to_use: Use this when you discover suspicious, gibberish-looking text ending in '=' or '==' during enumeration (e.g., in cookies, hidden form fields, or config files).

title: URL Encode a String (Python)
description: Uses Python's standard library to URL-encode a string, ensuring special characters (like spaces, &, and =) are safely transmitted within an HTTP GET request.
when_to_use: Essential when manually constructing complex SQL injection or Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) payloads in the terminal to avoid breaking the HTTP request syntax.
